target.name: Unspun
target.founder: Beth Esponnette, Walden Lam, Kevin Martin

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $57M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: SendCutSend
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Unspun is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company founded by Beth Esponnette, Walden Lam, and Kevin Martin that has raised $57M to date. The comparable universe spans 9 private robotics firms (including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity) and 1 public peer, providing sector context though limited strict comparability.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable valuation framework. Using total capital raised ($57M) as a value proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A progression yields an implied valuation of $114M. A peer-median approach was not viable given insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow robotics manufacturing cohort—the dataset contained only 2 adjacent-stage peers, below the 3-point minimum threshold for reliable triangulation. Relying on the broader sector median would introduce unacceptable noise.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $97M (low) to $131M (high), with a median point of $114M. This range reflects reasonable variance around the stage-progression methodology, though interpretation should be weighted toward the median given the single-method foundation. Key uncertainties include lack of true peer density at Unspun's stage and valuation tier, limiting pressure-testing against market multiples. The range should be treated as directional pending closer commercial data or emergence of tighter comparables.
